content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 14, 2001

Thomas M. Garrett, Ph.D.
MCP Industries, Inc.
Post Office Box 1839
Corona, CA 92878-1839

Re: Your Request for Informal Advice
Our File No. I-01-025

Dear Dr. Garrett:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 You are affiliated with a California business that is interested in hiring track coaches who may work for public schools. You have forwarded a draft employment agreement for our review and have asked us to advise you regarding the California laws governing the contract as it relates to potential conflicts these coaches may face. While we appreciate your efforts on their behalf, the Commission is prohibited from giving advice to third parties regarding the obligations of public officials. (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A).) Moreover, the Commission interprets only the Act and therefore is not in a position to advise the public on other potentially relevant legal obligations. Finally, several of the questions you pose require the expertise of legal counsel employed by you in the context of an attorney/client relationship. We make no endorsement regarding whom you should consider to advise your company. We note, however, that the California Political Attorney's Association may best be able to refer you to someone who can answer your questions. Peter Bagatilos of the CPAA may be reached by calling (415) 782-7100.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: C. Scott Tocher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
